data.title: UBS takeover of Credit Suisse pushed up Swiss banks' funding costs, SNB says
data.description:
Swiss banks have to pay more currently to secure liquidity in the financial markets than two years ago, the Swiss National Bank said on Thursday, pointing to the 2023 collapse of Credit Suisse and takeover by rival UBS as one explanation.

Between mid-2023 and end-2024, banks' funding costs increased significantly for bonds and in the money market, SNB governing board member Petra Tschudin said at an event in Geneva.

These costs have recently declined somewhat, but they remain elevated, which can affect the pricing and granting of loans, she added.

One reason for the higher funding costs is the state-engineered merger of UBS and Credit Suisse in March 2023, which increased the number of customers turning to domestically focused banks, according to the SNB.

Customers who previously did business with both UBS and Credit Suisse looked for additional banking relationships to reduce their reliance on a single big bank, Tschudin said.

This has meant more customers switching to Swiss domestic banks, pushing up demand in the Swiss capital market, especially as local banks have less access to international borrowing.

The shift to smaller banks may have increased due to UBS not offering the same conditions as Credit Suisse, Tschudin said.

"Unlike the former Credit Suisse, many of the domestically focused banks have little or no presence abroad, so their investor base is narrower."

"This has led to a corresponding rise in domestic funding needs," she said, a situation which had pushed up funding costs as banks compete for liquidity.

Other factors included a rise in government bond yields as central banks bought less government debt and a more challenging liquidity environment in general.

"In summary, bank funding costs, as measured by swap spreads, have risen noticeably in recent quarters," Tschudin said.

She also said despite the rise in funding costs, credit growth in Switzerland remains robust.

"Our monetary policy thus remains effective," Tschudin said.

data.title: Winvest Group Ltd SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Winvest Group Ltd, a company specializing in content creation and advertising services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational developments, reflecting the company's strategic initiatives and market activities.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $5,250 for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $0 for the same period in 2024, representing an increase due to contracts with BioCalth International Inc and Roundtable of Chinese.
  • Gross (loss)/profit: $(2,062) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $0 for the same period in 2024, primarily due to higher costs incurred for projects.
  • Loss from operations: $(300,147) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(153,137) for the same period in 2024, reflecting increased operating expenses.
  • Net loss: $(305,445) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(358,081) for the same period in 2024, representing a decrease in net loss due to reduced other expenses.
  • Basic and diluted (loss) per common share: $(0.00) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, consistent with the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported an increase in revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to contracts with BioCalth International Inc and Roundtable of Chinese. This indicates a focus on expanding service offerings in content creation and advertising services.
  • Cost of Revenue: The cost of revenue increased for the three months ended September 30, 2025, due to new projects undertaken, highlighting the company's investment in expanding its service delivery capabilities.
  • Operating Expenses: There was a significant increase in operating expenses for the three months ended September 30, 2025, driven by advertising expenses, loss on issuance of promissory notes, and bad debt expenses. This reflects the company's efforts to enhance its market presence and manage financial instruments.
  • Other Expenses: The company experienced a decrease in other expenses for the three months ended September 30, 2025, primarily due to the absence of a loss on investment that was present in the previous year. This suggests improved management of investment activities.
  • Future Outlook: Management is actively pursuing additional equity financing and managing operating costs prudently. The company is also advancing film production projects to generate future revenue streams, indicating a strategic focus on diversifying revenue sources and improving liquidity.

SEC Filing: Winvest Group Ltd [ WNLV ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: FDCTECH, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
FDCTECH, INC., a company specializing in investment and brokerage services, wealth management, and technology and software development, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report reveals significant financial and operational improvements, underscoring the company's strategic growth and enhanced efficiency.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $17.32 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous period's $18.18 million, indicating a decline of over 4.75% year-over-year.
  • Gross Profit: $9.45 million, showing an improvement from the previous year's $7.23 million, highlighting increased efficiency in cost management.
  • Operating Income: $0.90 million, compared to an operating loss of $1.69 million in the previous year, demonstrating a significant turnaround in operational performance.
  • Net Income: $0.44 million, a positive shift from a net loss of $0.86 million in the prior year, indicating improved profitability.
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.00, consistent with the previous period, reflecting the impact of a large number of shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in three primary business segments: Investment and Brokerage, Wealth Management, and Technology and Software Development. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, Investment and Brokerage generated $8,938,912, Wealth Management generated $4,976,601, and Technology and Software Development generated $3,400,210.
  • Geographical Performance: The company has expanded its global footprint through acquisitions, with key subsidiaries operating in Australia, Malta, the UK, and Cyprus. Alchemy Markets Ltd. (AML) in Malta and Alchemy Prime Ltd. (APL) in the UK are significant contributors to the company's brokerage services, with AML authorized to operate in multiple European countries.
  • New Product Launches: The company is developing the Condor Investing & Trading App, a simplified trading platform for stocks, ETFs, and other financial markets, expected to be commercialized by the end of the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • Future Outlook: Management plans to continue expanding its global customer base and enhancing its technological offerings. The company may seek external financing to support strategic growth initiatives, particularly in financial technologies.
  • Operational Efficiency: The company has demonstrated improved operational efficiency, with operating expenses decreasing as a percentage of total revenue over the past fiscal years.
  • Strategic Acquisitions: The company completed the acquisition of Alchemy Markets Ltd. and Alchemy Prime Ltd., enhancing its presence in the European market and expanding its service offerings in investment and brokerage.

SEC Filing: FDCTECH, INC. [ FDCT ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: ELECTRO SENSORS INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Electro Sensors Inc., a leading provider of industrial automation and OEM solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial growth and operational developments, reflecting the company's strategic initiatives and market expansion efforts.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: Electro Sensors Inc. reported net sales of $2.748 million for Q3 2025, marking a 9.4% increase from Q3 2024. This growth was primarily driven by increased sales to industrial automation and OEM customers.

Gross Profit: The company achieved a gross profit of $1.458 million for Q3 2025, a 15.3% increase over Q3 2024. The gross margin improved to 53.1% from 50.4%, attributed to higher average selling prices.

Operating Income: Operating income for Q3 2025 was $0.181 million, a slight increase from $0.173 million in Q3 2024. This reflects higher sales offset by increased operating expenses.

Net Income: Net income for Q3 2025 was $0.208 million, a decrease from $0.238 million in Q3 2024. The decline was primarily due to higher operating expenses and lower interest income.

Net Income per Share: The net income per share for Q3 2025 was $0.06, compared to $0.07 for Q3 2024, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The increase in net sales for the three and nine-month periods was primarily driven by increased sales to industrial automation and OEM customers.

Geographical Performance: Electro Sensors Inc. markets its products to customers located throughout the United States, Canada, Latin America, Europe, and Asia.

Future Outlook: The company continues to seek growth opportunities through its existing portfolio and potential acquisitions. It is also addressing supply chain challenges by seeking additional sources for components and modifying product designs to accommodate new components.

SEC Filing: ELECTRO SENSORS INC [ ELSE ] - 10-Q - Nov. 13, 2025

data.title: Alstom Posts Higher Adjusted Earnings on Commercial Momentum
data.description:
(17:00 GMT) *Alstom Saw Excellent Commercial Momentum
(17:00 GMT) *Alstom: All Product Lines Contributed to Strong Sales Growth
(17:01 GMT) *Alstom 1H Sales EUR9.06B
(17:01 GMT) *Alstom: Analysts Saw 1H Sales at EUR8.97B
(17:02 GMT) *Alstom 1H Adj EBIT EUR580M
(17:02 GMT) *Alstom: Analysts Saw 1H Adj EBIT at EUR569M
(17:02 GMT) *Alstom 1H Adj EBIT Margin 6.4%
(17:02 GMT) *Alstom: Analysts Saw 1H Adj EBIT Margin at 6.4%
(17:02 GMT) *Alstom 1H Orders EUR10.47B
(17:02 GMT) *Alstom: Analysts Saw 1H Orders at EUR10.60B
(17:03 GMT) *Alstom 1H Adj Net Profit EUR338M
(17:03 GMT) *Alstom: Analysts Saw 1H Adj Net Profit at EUR296M
(17:04 GMT) *Alstom Targets FY Sales Organic Growth Above 5%
(17:04 GMT) *Alstom Had Seen Sales Organic Growth Between 3% and 5% for FY
(17:05 GMT) *Alstom Targets FY Adj EBIT Margin Around 7%
(17:05 GMT) *Alstom Raises FY Organic Sales Growth Target
(17:05 GMT) *Alstom Backs Mid-Term Targets
(17:23 GMT) Alstom Posts Higher Adjusted Earnings on Commercial Momentum

By Nina Kienle

Alstom reported higher adjusted earnings before interest and taxes, driven by commercial momentum in North America.

The French train maker on Thursday said adjusted net profit for the first six month of the year rose to 338 million euros ($391.9 million) from 224 million euros in the prior-year period, while revenue rose 3% to 9.06 billion euros.

Adjusted earnings before interest and taxes--the company's key metric--grew 13% to 580 million euros, while its margin expanded to 6.4% from 5.9%.

The figures beat analysts' forecast of 569 million euros in adjusted EBIT from sales of 8.97 billion euros, according to a company-provided consensus.

Strong sales growth across all product lines demonstrated Alstom's ability to accelerate backlog delivery, Chief Executive Henri Poupart-Lafarge said.

Alstom raised its full-year organic sales growth target to above 5% from the previous range of 3% to 5%. It continues to target an adjusted EBIT margin of around 7%.

The company backed its midterm targets.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: PLATTS: 100--OIL FUTURES: Crude holds higher despite larger-than-expected US stock build
data.description: (Platts)--13Nov2025/1223 pm EST/1723 GMT **US crude stocks climb 6.41 million barrels **Cushing stocks down 350,000 barrels **EIA forecasts rising global oil inventories until 2026 A larger-than-expected US inventory build added headwinds to an early session crude oil price rally, but prices remained in positive territory in midday New York trading Nov. 13. At 1714 GMT, NYMEX December WTI was up 41 cents at $58.90/b and ICE January Brent traded 48 cents higher at $63.19/b. US commercial crude stocks climbed 6.41 million barrels to 427.58 million barrels in the week to Nov. 7, US Energy Information Administration data showed Nov. 13. The build exceeded recent market expectations after the American Petroleum Institute on Nov. 12 reported US crude oil stocks rose 1.3 million barrels over the period. The increase pushed US inventories to 24-week highs but left them still 4% behind the five-year average for this time of year. Notably, inventories at the NYMEX delivery hub in Cushing, Oklahoma, dipped 350,000 barrels to 22.52 million barrels. NYMEX December RBOB climbed 1.74 cents to $1.9728/gal while December ULSD declined 1.98 cents to $2.4618/gal. In its latest Short-Term Energy Outlook released Nov. 12, the EIA continued to project decreased crude oil prices through the end of 2025 and into 2026, as it expects rising inventories and global production to keep outpacing demand growth. The agency projects global oil inventories to increase by an average of 2.2 million b/d in 2026, compared with 1.8 million b/d in 2025. — Christopher Vanmoessner, christopher.vanmoessner@spglobal.com-- Edited by Benjamin Morse, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: The Crypto IPOs Keep Coming. Grayscale Is Next Up. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Paul R. La Monica

The crypto IPO boom isn't over yet.

Digital asset management firm Grayscale Investments filed for an initial public offering on Thursday, paving the way for it to join stablecoin company Circle Internet Group and brokerage firms Gemini Space Station, Bullish, and Galaxy Digital as newly public crypto companies on Wall Street.

Grayscale filed confidentially for its IPO with the Securities and Exchange Commission in July, allowing it to not publicly disclose its financials at the time. But now that Grayscale has officially filed for its IPO — and its books are open for everyone to see.

Grayscale's financial picture is mixed.

The good news? Grayscale, unlike many other fintech start-up firms, is profitable. The company, which managed $35 billion in assets as of Sept. 30, reported net income of $203.3 million in the first nine months of 2025.

But profitability, along with revenue, has decreased in the past year. Grayscale reported net income of $223.7 million during the first three quarters of 2024. Revenue through Sept. 30 came in at $318.7 million, down 20% from $397.9 million in the same period of 2024.

Grayscale, like other crypto firms, has been hit by the massive volatility in the market, with the prices of Bitcoin, Ethereum, and other digital coins rising and falling dramatically this year. Shares of Circle and Bullish have plunged from their post-IPO peak prices, while Gemini is trading for less than half of its offering price as investors fret about mounting losses due to rising expenses.

Grayscale noted in its IPO filing that the firm has "concentrated exposure to the digital asset industry, which may be exacerbated by our principal investments, and the success of our business is closely tied to conditions in the digital asset markets, which continue to be extremely volatile."

To that end, the Grayscale Bitcoin Trust exchange-traded fund, which was up as much as 33% in 2025 as recently as early October, is now up just 8.5% for the year following a recent crypto slide. And the Grayscale Ethereum ETF has given up virtually all of its gains in recent months. It is now flat in 2025 after surging more than 40% for the year at its peak in late August.

It will be interesting to see how Grayscale stock performs once it begins trading. No timeline for the IPO has been set yet. The company will first need to go on its roadshow with institutional investors and set a price range for its shares.

The company plans to trade on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol GRAY. Morgan Stanley, BofA Securities, Jefferies, and Cantor are the lead underwriters on the deal.

Write to Paul R. La Monica at paul.lamonica@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Pfizer Completes Acquisition of Metsera
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Pfizer closed its acquisition of weight-loss drug startup Metsera in a deal that could be valued at more than $10 billion.

Under the deal, Pfizer paid $65.60 a share in cash up front, and could additionally pay a contingent value right of up to $20.65 a share tied to specific clinical and regulatory milestones, the pharmaceutical company said Thursday.

Metsera is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer, and its shares will stop trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market after market close, Pfizer said.

The deal marks an investment in the future of medicine, according to Pfizer.

"By acquiring Metsera, we are directing our resources toward one of the most impactful and high-growth therapeutic areas and positioning ourselves to define it," Pfizer Chief Executive Albert Bourla said.

The deal follows a heated bidding war with rival Novo Nordisk. In September, Pfizer had agreed to buy Metsera for up to $7.3 billion, which Novo Nordisk topped with an unsolicited bid valued at up to $9 billion.

After Pfizer reached its deal with Metsera last week, Novo Nordisk said it wouldn't increase its offer because of its commitment to financial discipline and shareholder value. The company said it will keep assessing opportunities for business development and acquisition.

Metsera, meanwhile, said it decided to take Pfizer's offer in part because of a call from the Federal Trade Commission warning about potential risks to proceeding with a Novo Nordisk deal.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Electromed Price Target Raised to $36.00/Share From $35.00 by Roth Capital
Redeye: Alzinova (Q3 review) - Regulatory progress in anticipation of a deal
data.description:
Redeye provides a research update following the Q3 report published by Alzinova earlier today. The report contained no major surprises, with financials in line with our expectations. Mainly, Alzinova achieved important regulatory milestones during the quarter as the company received FDA approval of its IND application for a phase II study and was granted a Fast Track Designation for ALZ-101.

Read more and download the Research Update.

Follow companies at Redeye to receive the latest equity research within Life Science and Technology.

This is a press release from Redeye - Research Powered Investment Banking. www.redeye.se/

data.title: Verizon to Cut About 15,000 Jobs — WSJ
data.description:
By Patience Haggin and Suzanne Vranica

Verizon Communications is planning to cut roughly 15,000 jobs, looking to reduce costs as it contends with increased competition for wireless service and home internet, according to people familiar with the matter.

The cuts, the largest ever for the carrier, are set to take place in the next week, the people said. The majority of the reduction is expected to be made through layoffs.

Verizon also plans to transition about 200 stores into franchised operations, which will shift employees off its payroll, one of the people said.

Verizon had about 100,000 employees as of February, according to securities filings.

Verizon, the largest U.S. telecommunications provider by subscriber base, faces a fierce battle for both wireless and home internet customers. It has lost crucial postpaid phone subscribers for three consecutive quarters. In April, it began offering a price-lock guarantee for a swath of customers, though competitors have also since pushed similar promotions.

In the most recent quarter, Verizon lost a net 7,000 postpaid phone connections, while Wall Street analysts had forecast a gain of 19,000. AT&T and T-Mobile have both been growing those subscribers.

Last month, Verizon named its lead independent director Daniel Schulman as its new chief executive officer. Schulman, a former CEO of PayPal and Virgin Mobile USA, has said he would aggressively reduce the company's entire cost base and take steps to reverse the customer losses.

The company has said it plans to exit or streamline legacy businesses that don't offer clear paths to profitability, and will look to be a more nimble organization.

"We have a tremendous amount of opportunity to be more efficient, to be scrappier," Schulman said last month in a call discussing the company's third-quarter results. "Cost reductions will be a way of life for us here."

Write to Patience Haggin at patience.haggin@wsj.com and Suzanne Vranica at Suzanne.Vranica@wsj.com

data.title: REG - GCP Infra Inv Ltd - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 4949H GCP Infrastructure Investments Ltd 13 November 2025  

13 November 2025

GCP Infrastructure Investments Limited

(the "Company" or "GCP Infra")

LEI: 213800W64MNATSIV5Z47

Transaction in own shares

GCP Infra today announces that pursuant to the general authority granted by shareholders of the Company at the annual general meeting on 13 February 2025 to make market purchases of its own ordinary shares, it repurchased 200,000 ordinary shares at a weighted average price of 71.24 pence per share, to be held in treasury, on 13 November 2025.

Date of Purchase

13 November 2025

Number of ordinary shares purchased

200,000

Highest price paid (p)

71.50

Lowest price paid (p)

71.10

Following this transaction, the Company has in issue 884,797,669 ordinary shares of which 49,059,236 are held in treasury.

For reporting purposes under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules the market should exclude any shares held in treasury and should use the figure of 835,738,433 voting rights when determining if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in the Company.

For further information, please contact:

Gravis Capital Management Limited

Philip Kent

Robyn MacHugh

Cameron Gardner

+44 (0)20 3405 8500

RBC Capital Markets

Matthew Coakes

Elizabeth Evans

+44 (0)20 7653 4000

Stifel Nicolaus Europe Limited

Edward Gibson-Watt

Jonathan Wilkes-Green

+44 (0)20 7710 7600

Burson Buchanan

Helen Tarbet

Nick Croysdill

Henry Wilson

+44 (0)20 7466 5000

Notes to Editors

GCP Infra is a closed-ended investment company and FTSE-250 constituent whose shares are traded on the main market of the London Stock Exchange. Its objective is to provide shareholders with regular, sustained, long-term distributions and to preserve capital over the long term by generating exposure to UK infrastructure debt and related and/or similar assets.

The Company primarily targets investments in infrastructure projects with long term, public sector-backed, availability-based revenues. Where possible, investments are structured to benefit from partial inflation protection. GCP Infra is advised by Gravis Capital Management Limited.

GCP Infra has been awarded with the London Stock Exchange's Green Economy Mark in recognition of its contribution to positive environmental outcomes.
